slug: 为什么续集几乎都不如第一部
title: 为什么续集几乎都不如第一部

outline.concept: The mystery credit: a first film gets free tension because the audience doesn't know the rules, the world, or what's coming. This 'credit' is spent by the end. Sequels start in debt — they have to manufacture new unknowns that feel contrived against what's already established.
outline.query: 第一次看 悬念 揭晓 未知 surprise twist 震撼

outline.concept: Character arc exhaustion: a well-constructed first film resolves a protagonist's internal conflict. The sequel faces a trap — either undo that growth (which cheapens the original) or graft on a new arc to a character who already reached completion (which feels unearned and arbitrary).
outline.query: 角色成长 续集 重复 reset character arc 变化 倒退

outline.concept: Formula amplification — studios identify what 'worked' in the original and amplify it. The quiet romance becomes a love triangle; the one spectacular set piece becomes five. What was seasoning becomes the main course, and the dish loses the balance that made it work.
outline.query: 续集 更大 更多 失衡 bigger more sequel 失望 场面

outline.concept: Creative handoff: the original film is driven by someone with a specific vision they fought to realize. By the sequel, that creator has either left or is constrained by 'don't break what works.' Execution replaces invention. A brand is being maintained, not a story being told.
outline.query: 换导演 换编剧 续集 风格不同 original creator 味道变了
